Steps:
1. Go to /signup
2. Enter name + email + password
3. Submit
4. Check email for confirmation link
5. Click link
Expected Result: Account created, email confirmed, redirected to /app/dashboard

Steps:
1. Go to /login
2. Enter email + password
3. Submit
Expected Result: Redirected to /app/dashboard with session active

Steps:
1. Go to /login
2. Click 'Continue with Google'
3. Select Google account
Expected Result: Redirected to /app/dashboard, session active

Steps: 1. In dashboard, click Sign out
Expected Result: Session cleared, redirected to /login. Accessing /app/* while logged out redirects to /login

Steps: 1. While logged out, navigate directly to /app/dashboard
Expected Result: Redirected to /login immediately

Steps: 1. While logged in, navigate to /login or /signup
Expected Result: Redirected to /app/dashboard

Steps:
1. Open /book?event=<slug>
2. Select a date that has slots
3. Select a time slot
4. Enter name + email
5. Review and confirm
Expected Result: Booking confirmed page shown. Row appears in Supabase bookings table with correct date/time/status=confirmed

Steps:
1. Host must have Google Calendar connected
2. Complete a booking (US-007)
Expected Result: Event appears in host's Google Calendar with correct time, title, Google Meet link

Steps:
1. Open /book?event=<slug> in browser with non-UTC timezone
2. Verify timezone selector shows local timezone
3. Slots shown in local timezone
Expected Result: All time slots display in visitor's local timezone. Booking stored in UTC equivalent

Steps:
1. Open booking page
2. Click timezone selector
3. Change to a different timezone
Expected Result: All displayed slots update to new timezone. Booking still saves correctly

Steps:
1. Open /book?event=<slug>&utm_source=linkedin&utm_medium=cpc&utm_campaign=test&gclid=abc123
2. Complete booking
Expected Result: Booking row in Supabase has utm_source=linkedin, utm_medium=cpc, utm_campaign=test, gclid=abc123

Steps:
1. Open /book?event=<slug>&utm_source=x&utm_medium=y&utm_campaign=z&utm_term=a&utm_content=b&gclid=c&fbclid=d&li_fat_id=e&ttclid=f&msclkid=g
2. Complete booking
Expected Result: All 10 UTM/click-ID fields populated in bookings table

Steps: 1. Open /book with a date in the past selected
Expected Result: No slots shown. Day visually disabled in calendar

Steps:
1. Block the entire day in host Google Calendar
2. Navigate to /book, select that date
Expected Result: No time slots available for that date

Steps:
1. Set min_notice_hours=24 on event type
2. Try to book a slot less than 24 hours from now
Expected Result: That slot should not appear as available

Steps:
1. Set max_bookings_per_day=2 on event type
2. Make 2 bookings on the same day
3. Try a 3rd booking on same day
Expected Result: 3rd booking slot should not appear available

Steps: 1. Submit more than 20 bookings in 1 hour from same IP
Expected Result: API returns 429 Too Many Requests error

Steps: 1. Try booking with mailinator.com email address
Expected Result: Booking rejected with 'disposable email not allowed' error

Steps:
1. Add 1-2 custom questions to event type
2. Go to /book?event=<slug>
3. Fill out name/email
4. Proceed to details step
Expected Result: Custom questions appear in form. Answers saved in booking

Steps:
1. Two browser tabs open same /book page
2. Both select same date + time
3. Both submit
Expected Result: Only one booking succeeds. Second gets an error or is assigned to different host

Steps:

Steps:

Steps: 1. Go to /embed?event=<slug>

Steps:

2. Click a future date with bookings on the calendar
Expected Result: Left column updates to show only that date's bookings

Steps:

Steps:

2. Change name and duration
3. Save

2. Confirm deletion

Expected Result: Returns to scheduling list without saving changes

Steps:
1. Open edit for event type
2. Select a date on the right-panel calendar
Expected Result: Slots load and show available/unavailable. Unavailable slots are greyed with ? icon

Steps:
1. Open edit, select date with some grey slots
2. Click a grey slot
Expected Result: Diagnostic panel shows reason (e.g. 'Calendar busy at this time' or 'Day disabled')

Steps:

2. Set Mon-Fri 9am-5pm, disable Sat/Sun
3. Save
Expected Result: /book?event=<slug> only shows weekday slots in 9-5 range

Steps:

2. Assign it to an event type
3. Save
Expected Result: Event type uses that schedule's availability. Changing schedule updates all events using it

Steps:

Steps:

Steps:

2. Book same event type 3 times
Expected Result: First booking: member A, second: member B, third: member A (least recently booked first)

2. Make several bookings
Expected Result: All bookings go to member with calendar connected. Member without skipped

User Story: Collective meeting — all must be free
Steps:
1. Create event type with collective scheduling, 2 members
2. Block member B's calendar for a slot
3. Book that slot
Expected Result: Slot unavailable if member B required. Shows fewer slots than individual
